title: alnus oblongifolia
intro: Alnus oblongifolia, also known as the Manchurian alder, is a species of deciduous tree native to East Asia, belonging to the birch family (Betulaceae). The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in medicine due to their rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Alnus oblongifolia have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, extracts, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
